title: Who Is Alireza Tangsiri, the Latest Iranian Commander Targeted by Israel?
summary: Israel said it killed the naval commander, Alireza Tangsiri, in an airstrike on Thursday morning. Iran has not commented.

title: How Kharg Island May Change the Trajectory of the Iran War
summary: Kharg Island exports 90 percent of Iran’s crude oil. It has also become a potential U.S. target. Peter Eavis, our Business reporter, examines how the small island in the Persian Gulf has become a strategic target with significant risks.

title: Trump’s Threats to Europe Put Its Leaders in a Double Bind Over Iran
summary: European politicians risk angering their voters if they join America’s war. Yet they could also face domestic upheaval if they take no action to reopen shipping routes that Iran has blocked and ease an energy crisis.

title: Republicans in Congress Fret Over Trump Administration’s Handling of Iran War
summary: G.O.P. lawmakers who have given the Trump administration wide latitude to wage war with no congressional input are growing frustrated as officials offer little detail about ground troops, cost or timeline.

title: A Landmark Verdict on the Danger of Social Media, and Trump’s Call to Punish ‘Rogue Judges’
summary: Plus, OnlyFans’ billionaire owner dies at 43.

title: Juries Take the Lead in the Push for Child Online Safety
summary: A pair of verdicts held social media companies accountable for harming young users, highlighting a growing backlash as Congress struggles to pass legislation.

title: Snapchat Investigated in Europe Over Child Safety Policies
summary: Regulators in Brussels accused the social media platform of maintaining a weak age-verification system, and steering younger users toward inappropriate experiences.

title: Is Big Tech Facing a Big Tobacco Moment?
summary: Back-to-back courtroom losses have put technology giants, including Meta and Google, in uncertain territory as they face lawsuits and bans on teen users.

title: The Social Media Addiction Trials: What to Know
summary: Landmark trials are testing a new legal strategy claiming that Meta, TikTok, Snap and YouTube caused personal injury through addictive products.

title: Some Judges See Risks in Fiery Opinions Warning of Threats to Democracy
summary: Federal judges are weighing strategies for how to respond to the high stakes, anonymous threats and politicized atmosphere of the Trump era.

title: Trump Calls for Law Cracking Down on Crime and ‘Rogue Judges’
summary: Ahead of the midterms, President Trump told a Republican gathering that he wants to go after repeat offenders and “rogue judges that are criminals.”

title: Trump to Delay Nominating New C.D.C. Director
summary: The administration has yet to find a candidate who aligns with Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s agenda while avoiding his unpopular stance on vaccines.

title: U.S. Military Kills 4 People in Boat Strike in Caribbean
summary: At least 163 people have been killed in the Trump administration’s campaign against suspected drug smuggling.

title: ICE Agents at Some Airports Begin Checking IDs in Security Lines
summary: It was unclear whether having agents helping with screening passengers would improve wait times. Some travelers expressed worries.

title: In California’s Central Valley, Latino Voters are Up for Grabs in 2026’s Elections
summary: As in other Hispanic areas of the country, voters shifted toward Republicans in 2024. But there are increasing signs that this was a blip more than a durable trend.

title: Wealthy Investors Target Foes of Clean Energy, Seeking Revenge
summary: Renewable energy leaders said their industry got “rolled” in President Trump’s tax bill. Now they’re fighting back, starting in Texas.

title: Schumer Pledges Democrats Will Restore Clean Energy Tax Credits
summary: Senator Chuck Schumer of New York, the minority leader, said Democrats would pursue an agenda to reduce energy costs if they win back control of Congress.

title: Savannah Guthrie Says Mother’s Abduction Was Probably a Ransom Attempt
summary: Her interview on the “Today” show came more than 50 days after her mother, Nancy Guthrie, was taken from her home near Tucson, Ariz.

title: Colleges Pressed to Remove Names of Epstein’s Friends From Buildings
summary: Students and others are asking universities, including Harvard and Ohio State, to take down the names of high-profile donors with connections to Jeffrey Epstein. They have not done so yet.

title: Immigration Slowdown Hits Every Metro Area in the U.S., Census Shows
summary: Large urban counties and the border were the most affected. And in three-quarters of U.S. counties, population growth either slowed or turned negative.

title: New York City’s Population Flat After Drop in Immigration
summary: Newly released census data shows that from June 2024 to July 2025, the number of new residents who were international immigrants dropped by 70 percent compared to a year before.

title: Chinese Education Influencer’s Sudden Death Prompts Grief, and Reflection
summary: The influencer, Zhang Xuefeng, was known for no-nonsense, some said cynical, advice about how to win in China’s educational rat race. He died at 41.

title: Cuban Patients Are Dying Because of U.S. Blockade, Doctors Say
summary: Cuban health care was once the pride of the island. Now the U.S. oil blockade is upending even basic medical care.

title: How U.S.A.I.D. Birth Control Meant for Africa Was Ruined
summary: The Trump administration had options for offloading contraceptives once destined for Africa, a newly obtained memo shows. Instead, it has let them collect dust and go bad.

title: Men of the Trump Administration, 2026
summary: Are Democrats ugly? Asking for a friend.

title: It’s Not Trump. It’s America.
summary: The country’s faith in its ability to shape the world to its liking is a much older malady.

title: Josh Johnson Ridicules the Army’s Opening to Older Recruits
summary: “Look, according to People magazine — and apparently the U.S. military — 42 is the new 35,” Johnson said on Tuesday’s “Daily Show.”

title: Mamdani Promised to Freeze the Rent. Now the Fight Begins.
summary: Nine board members, six of whom were appointed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, will meet Thursday to begin weighing whether to enact a rent freeze.

title: Storms Could Bring Tornadoes, Hail and Heavy Rain to the Midwest on Thursday
summary: Forecasters were watching an area just south of the Great Lakes, where storms were expected to move through on Thursday afternoon and evening.

title: Leon Radvinsky, 43, Dies; Built the Adult-Entertainment Giant OnlyFans
summary: By leveraging social media and the influencer economy, he turned his website into a byword for online pornography in the 21st century.

title: Paul Klee’s “Angelus Novus” New York Debut On Hold Due to Mideast War
summary: The rarely seen “Angelus Novus” by Paul Klee was supposed to arrive at New York’s Jewish Museum, but remains in Israel instead.
